message:
fix(tests): adjust hook_denial color test to match current behavior

The FORCE_COLOR/CLICOLOR_FORCE env vars don't work with dcg's
should_use_color() which uses io::stderr().is_terminal() as final check.
Adjusted test to verify highlighting structure instead of ANSI output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -276,7 +276,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.4","title":"[Span] Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands","description":"# Task: Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands

## Background
DCG normalizes commands (wrapper stripping) and sanitizes safe spans.
If matching uses the normalized/sanitized string, highlight spans can drift
from the original command shown to users.

## Scope
- Ensure highlighted spans refer to the **original** command text
- Handle cases where normalization changes offsets

## Approach
- Track mapping between original and normalized/sanitized command when matches
  are computed on modified strings.
- If mapping is complex, compute match on the original command when safe
  (e.g., re-run regex on original for span only).
- Document fallback behavior when mapping is impossible.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/normalize.rs`
- `src/context.rs` / `src/trace.rs` (if mapping metadata stored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Highlighted spans point to correct substring in original command
- No regression in decision logic (matching stays as before)
- Edge cases documented (e.g., when only preview is available)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.5","title":"[Span] Unit tests for span capture + mapping","description":"# Task: Unit tests for span capture + mapping

## Purpose
Validate that every match source (pack/legacy/heredoc) produces a correct span
in original-command coordinates.

## Requirements
- Tests for pack regex span capture
- Tests for normalization/sanitization offset mapping
- Tests for heredoc/AST span mapping or fallback behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover multiple match sources
- Failures include detailed diagnostics of expected vs actual spans

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.6","title":"[Span] Unit tests for highlight formatter (UTF-8 + windowing + TTY)","description":"# Task: Unit tests for highlight formatter (UTF-8 + windowing + TTY)

## Purpose
Ensure highlight formatting is correct across UTF-8 input, long commands, and
TTY/non-TTY output modes.

## Requirements
- UTF-8 boundary cases (multi-byte chars)
- Long command windowing with ellipsis
- Non-TTY output contains no ANSI escapes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests validate caret alignment and output length
- Tests fail with clear diffs when formatting shifts

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.7","title":"[Span] E2E tests for hook output highlighting + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for hook output highlighting + verbose logs

## Purpose
Ensure end-to-end hook output includes span highlighting with detailed logging
so regressions are obvious.

## Requirements
- Extend `scripts/e2e_test.sh` (or add new E2E script) to cover:
  - A blocked command with highlighting
  - Non-TTY mode (no ANSI)
  - Long command windowing
- Include verbose logs on failure to show full output

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests validate caret line positioning
- Logs include full stderr output for debugging

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","title":"[PACK] dns.cloudflare - Cloudflare DNS Pack","description":"# Cloudflare DNS Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Cloudflare DNS operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Wrangler CLI
- `wrangler dns-records delete`
- Zone purge operations

### API
- `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../dns_records/`
- `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../zones/`

### Terraform
- `terraform destroy` on cloudflare_record resources

## Safe Patterns
- `wrangler dns-records list`
- `curl -X GET` to Cloudflare API
- `wrangler whoami`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: dns.* (git_safety_guard-x3g)
- Keywords: wrangler, cloudflare
- Note: Zone deletion affects all records

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DNS record deletion blocked
- [ ] Zone deletion blocked
- [ ] API patterns detected
- [ ] Tests cover common Cloudflare operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:33:48.996879217-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:20:17.966286832-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:20:17.966286832-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented dns.cloudflare pack + registry + e2e tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.681210508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.724429275-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.770402474-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.813135817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.855065759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.899342757-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.459747934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.50597559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-k884","title":"[Metrics] Create analytics queries by rule_id","description":"# Task: Create analytics queries by rule_id

## Parent Epic: Rule-Level Metrics (git_safety_guard-1dri)

## Objective

Implement database queries that aggregate command history by rule_id to provide per-rule metrics.

## Metrics to Calculate

### Per-Rule Aggregation
- Total hits (denials)
- Total allowlist overrides
- Override percentage (noise indicator)
- First seen timestamp
- Last seen timestamp
- Unique command count

### Time-Based Trends
- Hits per day/week
- Trend direction (increasing/decreasing)

### Cross-Rule Comparisons
- Most triggered rules
- Noisiest rules (highest override rate)
- Most effective rules (lowest override rate)

## Query Interface

In src/history/schema.rs or src/history/analytics.rs:

pub struct RuleMetrics {
    pub rule_id: String,
    pub total_hits: u64,
    pub allowlist_overrides: u64,
    pub override_rate: f32,
    pub first_seen: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub last_seen: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub unique_commands: u64,
    pub trend: Trend,
}

pub enum Trend {
    Increasing,
    Stable,
    Decreasing,
}

impl HistoryDb {
    pub fn get_rule_metrics(&self, since: Option<DateTime<Utc>>) 
        -> Result<Vec<RuleMetrics>, HistoryError>;
    
    pub fn get_rule_metrics_for_rule(&self, rule_id: &str) 
        -> Result<Option<RuleMetrics>, HistoryError>;
    
    pub fn get_noisiest_rules(&self, limit: usize) 
        -> Result<Vec<RuleMetrics>, HistoryError>;
}

## SQL Queries

-- Per-rule aggregation
SELECT 
    rule_id,
    COUNT(*) as total_hits,
    SUM(CASE WHEN outcome = 'AllowedByAllowlist' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as overrides,
    MIN(timestamp) as first_seen,
    MAX(timestamp) as last_seen,
    COUNT(DISTINCT command) as unique_commands
FROM commands
WHERE rule_id IS NOT NULL
  AND timestamp > ?
GROUP BY rule_id
ORDER BY total_hits DESC;

## Trend Calculation

Compare recent period to previous period:

recent_hits = hits in last 7 days
previous_hits = hits in 7-14 days ago
trend = (recent_hits - previous_hits) / previous_hits

## Acceptance Criteria

- All metrics calculated correctly
- Queries are efficient (use indexes)
- Time filtering works
- Trend calculation is reasonable

## Dependencies

- Task: Ensure rule_id captured

## Estimated Effort: 1 day","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:10:45.03222106-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:10:45.03222106-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-k884","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-hmag","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:10:53.253557865-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-k884","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1dri.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:21:26.665788291-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

- [ ] Unit tests for project/environment deletion

- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/featureflags_launchdarkly.txt`

- [ ] Project deletion blocked
- [ ] List/get operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:42.8329088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:42:47.241179686-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:38:09.635255254-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.181287496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.224883022-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.267010766-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.312213085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.356939047-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.401171782-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.683734031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.7315613-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-wb2m","title":"[Suggest] Implement pattern generation from clusters","description":"# Task: Implement conservative pattern generation from clusters

## Parent Epic: Auto-Suggested Allowlists (git_safety_guard-5apz)

## Objective
Given a cluster of similar commands, generate a regex pattern that matches all
commands while staying **as specific as possible**. Avoid broad patterns that
could allow destructive variants.

## Pattern Generation Strategy
- Prefer token anchoring and explicit alternation over wildcards
- Avoid `.*` unless the risk score is LOW and the segment is clearly non-destructive
- Use `\b` and whitespace anchors to prevent partial matches

### Example
Commands: ["npm run build", "npm run test", "npm run lint"]
Pattern: `^npm\s+run\s+(build|test|lint)\b`

### Variable Segments
- Enumerate known values when count is small
- Use tight classes only when value form is obvious (UUID, hex, numeric)
- Never generalize paths or flags unless they are identical across cluster

## Implementation
1. Find common prefix and suffix
2. Segment token stream
3. Classify each variable segment (enumeration vs constrained pattern)
4. Build regex with anchors
5. Validate the generated regex against all cluster members

## Data Structures
```
pub struct GeneratedPattern {
    pub regex: String,
    pub specificity_score: f32,
    pub matches_all: bool,
    pub example_matches: Vec<String>,
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generated patterns match all cluster commands
- Patterns are specific, anchored, and avoid broad wildcards
- Tests include negative cases showing non-matching destructive variants

## Dependencies
- Command clustering algorithm

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-wfr","title":"[PACK] payment.stripe - Stripe Pack","description":"# Stripe Payment Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Stripe CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Stripe CLI
- `stripe webhook_endpoints delete`
- `stripe customers delete`
- `stripe products delete`
- `stripe prices delete`
- `stripe coupons delete`
- `stripe api_keys roll` (key rotation without coordination)

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE api.stripe.com/v1/`

## Safe Patterns
- `stripe listen` (webhook testing)
- `stripe customers list`
- `stripe products list`
- `stripe payments list`
- `stripe logs tail`
- API GET requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: payment.* (git_safety_guard-atw)
- Keywords: stripe
- Note: Webhook deletion breaks payment notifications

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Webhook deletion blocked
- [ ] Customer deletion blocked

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-woe","title":"Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests","description":"# Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests

## Background

DCG runs as a hook on EVERY Bash command executed by Claude Code. In a typical coding session:
- Hundreds to thousands of commands are executed
- Each command invokes DCG
- Memory leaks accumulate across invocations
- Long sessions could exhaust system memory

CASS has dedicated memory leak tests that run in CI to catch leaks early. DCG needs the same.

## Why This Matters for DCG Specifically

### Usage Pattern
```
User starts Claude Code session
  → Claude executes: git status        → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: ls -la            → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: cargo build       → DCG invoked
  → ... (repeat 500+ times)            → DCG invoked each time
Session ends after hours
```

Even small leaks (a few KB per invocation) compound:
- 1000 invocations × 1KB leak = 1MB leaked
- Long session with 5000 invocations = 5MB leaked
- Multiple concurrent sessions = problem

### Critical Code Paths

These DCG components process every command and must not leak:

1. **Input parsing** (`hook.rs`)
   - JSON parsing with serde_json
   - String allocation for command extraction

2. **Pattern matching** (`evaluator.rs`)
   - Regex compilation (lazy_static, but still)
   - Match result allocation

3. **Heredoc processing** (`heredoc.rs`)
   - Content extraction
   - Script parsing
   - AST construction

4. **Output generation** (`trace.rs`, `hook.rs`)
   - JSON serialization
   - Colorful warning formatting

## Technical Approach

### Test Infrastructure

Create `tests/memory_tests.rs` with:

```rust
//! Memory leak detection tests
//! 
//! These tests process many inputs and check that memory usage
//! doesn't grow unboundedly. They must run with --test-threads=1
//! to get accurate measurements.

use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

/// Custom allocator that tracks allocations
struct TrackingAllocator;

static ALLOCATED: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);

unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator {
    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.alloc(layout)
    }
    
    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.dealloc(ptr, layout)
    }
}

fn memory_usage() -> usize {
    ALLOCATED.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
}
```

### Test Cases

1. **Hook input parsing** - Parse 1000 JSON inputs, check memory stable
2. **Pattern evaluation** - Evaluate 1000 commands, check memory stable
3. **Heredoc extraction** - Process 1000 heredocs, check memory stable
4. **Full pipeline** - End-to-end 1000 times, check memory stable

### Memory Budget

Tests should assert:
- Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- No single iteration leaks > 10KB
- Final memory within 10% of baseline after GC

## Implementation Tasks

1. **Create memory test infrastructure** - Tracking allocator, helpers
2. **Add hook parsing memory test** - JSON input processing
3. **Add pattern matching memory test** - Evaluator path
4. **Add heredoc processing memory test** - Script extraction
5. **Add memory tests to CI workflow** - Dedicated job

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Memory tests exist and pass locally
- [ ] Memory tests run in CI (dedicated job)
- [ ] Tests catch intentionally introduced leaks
- [ ] Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- [ ] Tests run with --test-threads=1 for accuracy

## Considerations

### Test Isolation
Memory tests must run sequentially (--test-threads=1) to get accurate measurements. Other tests running concurrently would pollute measurements.

### CI Resources
Memory tests should run on a dedicated CI job to:
- Avoid interference with other tests
- Have predictable resource allocation
- Allow longer timeouts if needed

### Platform Differences
Memory tracking may behave differently on Linux vs macOS. Focus on Linux (CI environment) but tests should pass everywhere.

### False Positives
Some "leaks" are actually intentional caches:
- Lazy static regex patterns (compile once, keep forever)
- Pack pattern registry (loaded once at startup)

Tests should account for expected one-time allocations.

## References

- CASS memory tests: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search/.github/workflows/bench.yml (memory-tests job)
- Rust tracking allocator: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/alloc/trait.GlobalAlloc.html","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:21:21.710163301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:48:31.321864332-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:48:31.321864332-05:00","close_reason":"All dependencies completed: memory test infrastructure (dj8), memory tests (39k), and CI job (8zo) are all closed; tests/memory_tests.rs exists and memory-tests job in CI workflow","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-dj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.375811198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-39k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.406752934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-8zo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.43467007-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-wso","title":"[CATEGORY] cdn.* - CDN & Edge Pack Suite","description":"# CDN & Edge Pack Suite

## Overview
CDN and edge services cache content globally. Cache purges and config changes affect all users. Origin configuration changes can cause global outages.

## Packs to Implement

### cdn.cloudflare
- **Why Critical**: Cloudflare provides CDN, security, and edge compute. Rule deletion or purge affects global traffic.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `wrangler delete` - Deletes Workers
  - `wrangler kv:key delete` / `kv:namespace delete`
  - Cache purge operations
  - Firewall rule deletion
  - Page rule deletion
- **Safe Patterns**: `wrangler whoami`, `list`, `get`

### cdn.fastly
- **Why Critical**: Fastly powers high-traffic sites. Service version activation can cause outages.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `fastly service delete`
  - `fastly domain delete`
  - `fastly backend delete`
  - VCL changes via API
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe`, `list`, `version list`

### cdn.cloudfront
- **Why Critical**: AWS CloudFront is deeply integrated with AWS services.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws cloudfront delete-distribution`
  - `aws cloudfront create-invalidation` (cache purge - costly)
  - Origin configuration changes
- **Safe Patterns**: `list-`, `get-`, `describe-`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major CDN providers covered
- [ ] Cache purge operations flagged
- [ ] Edge compute deletion detected
- [ ] Configuration changes handled","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Every cdn.* pack must comply with Standard Pack Task Requirements (git_safety_guard-ikf2), including unit tests and e2e corpus scenarios.
